notes: Anonymous associative horizontal dimension block reading 700 mm; DIM layer + DIMDOT + DEFPOINTS, no wall geometry.

rationale.evidence: All 9 entities lie on DIM (6) and DEFPOINTS (3) layers: two vertical extension lines (8EF8, 8EF9) and a horizontal dimension line (8EFA) whose span equals 700, matching the single MTEXT 8EFD '\A1;700' (=700 mm). Two DIMDOT INSERT arrow-heads (8EFB, 8EFC) and three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8EFE, 8EFF, 8F00) complete the associative-dimension signature.
rationale.rule: Anonymous '*D####' block-table definitions whose entities are entirely DIM-layer LINEs plus DIMDOT terminator INSERTs, DEFPOINTS POINTs, and one measurement MTEXT equal to the extension-line span are AutoCAD associative dimension caches, not drawn geometry; this holds in any AutoCAD drawing.
rationale.counterfactual: If those LINEs were on a wall/architectural layer and lacked the DIMDOT arrows, DEFPOINTS points, and the 700-valued MTEXT, the block would read as wall linework and wall_likelihood would rise toward 1.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection shows the measurement but not the real edge it annotates, so whether 700 mm is a wall length, an opening, or a clear dimension cannot be resolved; either way the block itself carries no wall line.

notes: Anonymous associative horizontal dimension block reading 215 mm (small-gap variant with split dimension line); DIM + DIMDOT + DEFPOINTS, no wall geometry.

rationale.evidence: This dimension has 11 entities (LINE=5): two vertical extension lines (8F5B, 8F5C) bound a 215-unit gap too small for internal arrows, so the dimension line is split into inner segment 8F5F plus outer stub segments 8F5D and 8F5E. Two DIMDOT INSERTs (8F60, 8F61), one MTEXT 8F62 '\A1;215' (=215 mm), and three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8F63-8F65) confirm an associative dimension.
rationale.rule: Anonymous '*D####' block-table definitions whose entities are entirely DIM-layer LINEs plus DIMDOT terminator INSERTs, DEFPOINTS POINTs, and one measurement MTEXT equal to the extension-line span are AutoCAD associative dimension caches, not drawn geometry; this holds in any AutoCAD drawing.
rationale.counterfactual: If those LINEs were on a wall/architectural layer and lacked the DIMDOT arrows, DEFPOINTS points, and the 215-valued MTEXT, the block would read as wall linework and wall_likelihood would rise toward 1.
rationale.uncertainty: 215 mm is consistent with an interior wall thickness, but the measured edge is not in the projection so this cannot be confirmed; the block still contains no wall line.

notes: Insulation (INSUL) pattern fragment of the basic-type floor plan (평면도 기본형): 159 uniform 5-vertex LWPOLYLINEs of wall-cavity insulation; no wall-face line.

rationale.evidence: All 159 entities are LWPOLYLINE on the single bound layer 'X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL' (handles 4376..4414), each with exactly 5 vertices - the fingerprint of a repeated insulation-batting fill, not long wall edges. The '$0$' token marks the layer as bound from an xref of the 평면도(기본형) plan, and 'INSUL' identifies it as insulation, i.e. wall-assembly material.
rationale.rule: A block-table definition made entirely of uniform LWPOLYLINEs on a plan's INSUL/material layer is wall-assembly insulation fill: it reliably marks where walls run yet its polylines are pattern segments, so it signals wall PRESENCE without yielding an extractable wall-FACE line. This transfers to any plan carrying a material sublayer.
rationale.counterfactual: If these were long 2-point straight LINE/LWPOLYLINE runs on a WALL/구조(structural) layer instead of 5-vertex INSUL pattern loops, they would read as wall faces and both wall_likelihood and wall_line_handles would jump.
rationale.uncertainty: Vertex coordinates are not in the projection, so the exact wall runs traced and whether they are exterior vs interior walls cannot be recovered; the role could alternatively be read as 심볼(패턴) or 기타 rather than 평면 부분도 since the block is single-layer pattern fill, not a composite plan chunk.
